existing_claim: Attacked the 'additive by supersession, never destructive' commitment for conflict with the schema's supersession model: §2.4 correctly describes per-node state-selected behavior (new business key created; changed versioned-ground-truth node supersedes on explicit version increment and retains the predecessor; unchanged introduces no new version; age/distill planes follow their own per-plane discipline) — this aligns with DDR-002 §6 Option A supersession (retained version nodes + effective-dating + `superseded_by`) and the never-delete posture, and does not assert an in-place overwrite the schema forbids. The commitment held against DDR-002's supersession and never-delete disciplines.
incoming_claim: Attacked 'additive by supersession, never destructive' (§2.4) for conflict with the schema's supersession and never-delete disciplines. It held: §2.4 describes per-node state-selected behavior — new business key created; a changed versioned-ground-truth node supersedes on explicit version increment and retains the predecessor; unchanged introduces no new version; age/distill planes follow their own per-plane discipline — which aligns with DDR-002 §6 Option A supersession (retained version nodes + effective-dating + superseded_by) and the DDR-002 §7 #8 never-delete posture, and asserts no in-place overwrite the schema forbids.
